ab: sa lrolraina naiya takaikaiya so Taromake, si atwanaw takesiyana, lra mwsali Taicyo, tara ikai maci tacekecekelra.
ch: 小時候我們家住在達魯瑪克，上學以後，就搬到了台中，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Taromake. After I started school, we moved to Taichung and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: na kelanga taikake, watakesiako eea ka takesi idra kakoza ki kacelisiya, athingale nakowa makaelraela kakoza ki kacalisiya so Taivang.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: amanisa, taisadrasadraza ki kaladraidraza, konako si takali ka sawalay mwbaliyowa cekeleli kielraelra lramarodrang, anatoma si alrecega yataka cekele kakozaneta.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: tara mavalolronga ka cayli ini kai marodrang, eene loceele takamaiya yacengeceng.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: lamani ko kielraelra idra wathingale taiya ka vagana, kali’ace ini sing Taromakana, miya maligilri swa Songaw, swa Talalibeka, swa Di’ong.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的東魯凱語說得很流利，也會說布農語、卑南語和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Taromak, as well as Bunun, Pinuyumayan and Japanese.

ab: la kielraelrako kwani na akithingale nakowa, si sa lrolrainga wakicakwa ini vaga ki swa Di’ong, ko sakacekele ini ka swa Songaw, mathingathingale takalra swa Talalibeka ikai tatwlavalavaza ini, amanisa malalekete kawriva makalra ka makaelraelra vagana.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是布農族人，而他工作上認識許多卑南族人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Bunun, and he knows many Pinuyumayan at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: lo tamasososo sakibolobolo nai,ko marodrang elraelraelra makalra kwadra sininadroma lrigila ki alro’o.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: eene odrava lo kadrowa makathingale kidremedreme nai, la ni arakaiya nai ki soci rako’ing, lai kalalekete nai kilalra twadriyadringai.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: sa lrolrainganako nako thingale kakoza ki cekeleta, maininoako ki sw kwacalisiya.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: sa wathingale arokakalra, lo kidremedremeli sw Taromakako ka nikalrivala, miya ka anikamiya.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為東魯凱族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Taromak is unique and something to be proud of.
